the terminal rates. The question
had been canVassed for weeks, the
council had been urged by news
papers and delegations of citizens
to stand pat for the concessions

Now comes the side of the rail
road 1t is urged that it means
the loss of the two roads, and the
loss of at least $5,000,000 that
would be invested in Spokane, the
loss of the proposed union depot,
the new city hall and other i'm
provements that were planned.


Said DR.. E. Pittwood: In the
last three months $1,500,000 of Spo-
kane money has gone to Portland
for investment, 100000 to Cali
fornia, 100000 to Vancouver and
100000 to other points. Now the
loss of these railroads adds $4,000
900 more. | will venture the asser-
ton that the loss of the North
Coast and the Milwaukee will cause
a depreciation of $10,000,000 in Spo-
kane real estate. Can we afford
to stand it? | think not.


"The action of the city council
does not look right to me. There
is something suspicious about it,
that will not bear investigation.
What power is responsible for the
turning down of the North Coast
and the Milwaukee? Let us get
at the truth"
